<!-- generated by sofar — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: sync-client

- 2026-07-12T13:23:22.670Z — chose **Build the v2 sync CLIENT inside packages/engine as src/client/ + the @alignlabs/sofar/client subpath, under the user's explicit Phase 5.1 task (the D14 seam; server = private sofar-cloud repo, its Phases 1-4 shipped)** over Treating the CLAUDE.md Fable-window guard-rail ("DO NOT build the sync service/backend") as blocking, or a separate packages/sync workspace because The guard-rail bans the service/backend, not the client of an already-shipped server; the task brief explicitly authorizes the client and mandates a subpath export of @alignlabs/sofar (Tauri shell + iOS consume it later), matching the existing /schema and /engine lib-surface laws (side-effect-free import, zero-runtime-deps manifest unchanged)
- 2026-07-12T13:23:28.601Z — chose **Storage split: committable .sofar/remote.json ({version, api_url, org, name, repo_id} — repo_id is not a secret, teammates share the binding); credentials in ~/.config/sofar/credentials.json (XDG_CONFIG_HOME-aware, 0600, keyed by normalized api_url); sync cursors per-clone in ~/.local/state/sofar/sync/<sha256(root)>.json (XDG_STATE_HOME-aware), invalidated when api_url/repo_id change** over Cursors in a committed .sofar file, in .git/ (absent under --root without git), or keyed by repo_id alone (two clones of one repo would share per-clone cursors) because Ack/pull cursors mutate on every sync — committing them dirties the repo per push and invites non-union JSON merge conflicts; they are per-clone facts about what THIS checkout exchanged with the server, while the org/repo binding is team-shared truth and belongs in the record dir; idempotent push/pull by event id makes a lost cursor safe (worst case re-push/re-pull, never loss)
- 2026-07-12T13:23:36.529Z — chose **Zero new dependencies: native fetch (Node >=18) for all HTTP, a hand-rolled minimal SSE reader for the doorbell, injectable fetch/sleep for tests** over Adding an HTTP client, eventsource, or retry library because CLAUDE.md dependency law (nothing beyond the four without a Decision) and the zero-runtime-deps manifest; the wire contract is small (JSON + NDJSON + one SSE endpoint with data/heartbeat lines) and the doorbell is notification-only, so a missed/malformed frame can never lose data — pull is the truth path
- 2026-07-12T13:46:33.400Z — chose **Amend SPEC §Architectural invariants: the zero-model-API-calls invariant stays absolute, and its "no user content sent anywhere" clause is scoped — the v2 sync client pushes record events to the user's OWN authenticated sofar-cloud repo, opt-in via sofar login + sofar link, and nothing else ever leaves the machine; a new SPEC §Sync client section carries the full client contract** over Leaving the invariant text as-is (sync would literally violate it) or weakening the invariant to allow arbitrary egress because SPEC is authoritative over code — shipping sync without amending it would make the record lie; the invariant's intent (felt-cost D3: no model calls, no API keys, no inference costs) is untouched, and sync egress is fenced to explicit, revocable, user-owned destinations
- 2026-07-12T16:23:02.190Z — chose **Canonical serializeEvent in the engine (0.9.1): byte form is a pure function of the envelope value — fixed schema order v,id,ts,initiative,session,source,actor,user?,type,payload; unknown additive envelope fields preserved after payload (sorted), never dropped; payload + nested objects code-point-sorted recursively, arrays in order; scalars via JSON.stringify (ts verbatim). Forward-only: existing lines never rewritten; pull writes the canonical form of the PARSED event, so a non-canonical server cannot poison a local log.** over Reordering sofar-cloud's rowToLine reconstruction to match the CLI's insertion order (the drift-class fix fold-parity forbids), or byte-parity-with-history at the payload level (impossible: jsonb loses insertion order server-side, and 971 of this repo's 2606 committed lines have unsorted payload insertion order, e.g. decision_logged {chose,over,because}) because events.jsonl is git-committed — writer and puller emitting different bytes for identical events means spurious diffs/merge conflicts (proven live in sofar-cloud's 5.2 drill). Envelope-level fixed order byte-matches 100% of committed history (swept all 2606 events: 0 envelope-order breaks, 0 semantic drift, idempotent everywhere); payload sorting is the only form both sides can reconstruct after jsonb reorders keys, and historical unsorted-payload lines stay untouched in place (append-only + dedupe-by-id) while export/push/pull legs re-serialize identically on both sides
